content:
theorem(case C): ramified p=5 Jordan filtration + one-page synthesis
Closes Case C of Part II with the clean algebraic identity:
  ε := F - 3I, then ε² = -5F + 10I = 5·(2I - F) = 5·η
  where det η = 1, so η ∈ GL₂(Z/5^k Z) for all k.
Hence ε behaves like a √5-nilpotent (valuation ½) in the ramified
extension Z_5[φ] / (x² - x - 1).

σ-orbits on L_0 of (Z/5^k Z)² — structure proven (k ≥ 2) and
verified empirically on k ∈ {1, 2, 3}:

  k = 1:  24 = 1×20 + 1×4            (eigenspace mod 5, ord(3) = 4)
  k = 2: 600 = 5×100 + 5×20          (5 × π(25) + 5 × π(5))
  k = 3:15000 = 25×500 + 25×100      (25 × π(125) + 25 × π(25))

For k ≥ 2: exactly two strata — generic (orbit length π(5^k)) and
depth-1 Jordan lift (orbit length π(5^{k-1})), each with 5^{k-1}
orbits. The k=1 eigenspace-orbit-of-length-4 does NOT appear on L_0
for k ≥ 2 (it moves to L_1 under the scaling isomorphism).

author: Player2
content:
theorem(part II): σ-only sub-stratification via Legendre (5
p) trichotomy|Adds Part II to QA_ORBIT_STRATIFICATION_THEOREM.md, classifying σ-alone
orbits within each L_j once μ is removed.

Structure (all within each level L_j, after reduction to L_0 of p^{k-j}):

  Case A — Inert (p=2 or (5|p)=-1, so p ∈ {2,3,7,13,17,23,...}):
    x²-x-1 irreducible over F_p. F has no F_p-eigenvectors.
    All L_0 orbits uniform at length π(p^n). Count = |L_0|/π(p^n).
    Verified on (p,n) ∈ {(2,3), (3,2), (3,3), (7,2), (13,1), (13,2)}.

  Case B — Split ((5|p)=+1, so p ∈ {11,19,29,31,...}):
    x²-x-1 = (x-φ)(x-ψ) over F_p with φψ = -1.
    F is diagonalisable; two 1D eigenspaces contribute
    (p-1)·p^{n-1}/ord(λ) orbits of length ord(λ) each.
    Remaining (non-eigenspace) vectors form generic orbits of length
    lcm(ord(φ), ord(ψ)) = π(p^n).
    Verified on m ∈ {11, 19, 29, 121}.
    Examples:
      mod 11: 1×10 (φ) + 2×5 (ψ) + 10×10 (generic)
      mod 19: 2×9 (φ) + 1×18 (ψ) + 18×18 (generic)
      mod 29: 2×14 (φ) + 4×7 (ψ) + 56×14 (generic)

  Case C — Ramified (p=5, discriminant of Fibonacci char poly):
    x²-x-1 has double root 3 mod 5. F is a Jordan block.
    Orbits split between "full-depth" (length π(p^n)) and
    "Jordan-reduced" (length π(p^{n-1})).
    Empirical on mod 25: 5×100 + 5×20 on L_0, 1×20 + 1×4 on L_1.
    Full Jordan-filtration classification deferred.

Composite m extends by CRT as in Proposition C: per-factor orbit
lengths combine via lcm; counts multiply.

Interpretation: μ is the generator that (i) identifies the two F_p
eigenspaces in Case B and (ii) mixes Jordan strata in Case C. Part I's
single component per L_j is what you get after μ does this collapsing.

This is the mathematically right sequel to the {σ,μ}-orbit theorem:
Part I pins down content-ideal classification; Part II gives the
Frobenius / Jordan refinement within each class.

author: Player2
content:
theorem: Proposition B proven via GL₂(Z/p^k Z) orbit classification
The Pisano-periodicity path was a red herring. The proof goes through
elementary linear algebra over the local ring Z/p^k Z:

1. σ and μ are matrix multiplications by F = [[0,1],[1,1]] and
   S = [[0,1],[1,0]], both in GL₂(Z).
2. SF = [[1,1],[0,1]] = T and FS = Tᵀ generate SL₂(Z) (Nielsen generators).
   Since det F = det S = −1, ⟨F, S⟩ = GL₂(Z).
3. The reduction map GL₂(Z) → GL₂(Z/p^k Z) is surjective.
   Hence ⟨σ, μ⟩ = GL₂(Z/p^k Z) as a transformation group on (Z/p^k Z)².
4. GL₂(R)-orbits on R² for a local principal ideal ring R are classified
   by the content ideal (b,e)·R = (p^j) where j = min(v_p(b), v_p(e)).
   By row-reduction, any pair with content (p^j) lands on (p^j, 0) via
   a GL₂(R) element; the content is GL₂(R)-invariant.
5. Therefore orbits are exactly the k+1 level sets L_j. ☐

Orbit-stabilizer corroboration: |GL₂(Z/p^k Z)| / |Stab((1,0))| = |L_0|
verified analytically on (p,k) ∈ {(2,3), (3,2), (5,2), (7,2)}.

Proposition C also promoted to a theorem — the same proof extends
through CRT: matrix multiplication commutes with CRT, so
⟨σ,μ⟩ on (Z/mZ)² factors as the product of GL₂ actions on each
prime-power factor. Orbits = Cartesian products of per-factor orbits.

This means: Props A/B/C hold for ALL primes p and ALL k ≥ 1. The earlier
empirical table (p ∈ {2, 3, 5, 7}) is corroboration, not the basis.

Still open: potential ν role, σ-only sub-stratification within each L_j
(related to Pisano period π(p^k)).

content:
docs(theory): Propositions A/B/C — prime-power invariant + CRT product
Replaces the CRT section with a cleaner three-part structure.

  Proposition A (prime-power invariant):
    On (Z/p^k Z)², the function J(b,e) = min(v_p(b), v_p(e)) is invariant
    under σ(b,e) = (e, qa_mod(b+e)) and μ(b,e) = (e,b). Proof by cases on
    whether v_p(b) = v_p(e). Takes values in {0, 1, ..., k}.

  Proposition B (level sets = components; empirical):
    The k+1 level sets L_j = {(b,e) : J(b,e) = j} are exactly the
    {σ,μ}-orbit components. Verified on (p,k) ∈ {(2,3), (3,2), (3,3)}.
    Closed-form sizes: |L_j| = p^{2(k-j-1)}·(p²-1) for j<k, |L_k| = 1.

    For m = Π p_i^{k_i}, the {σ,μ}-decomposition on (Z/mZ)² is the
    Cartesian product of the per-factor Proposition-B decompositions.
    Total component count = Π (k_i + 1).
    Empirically verified on m ∈ {15, 24, 45, 72} — component counts AND
    sizes match predicted products exactly.

author: Player2
content:
docs(theory): retract "doesn't generalize" — it's CRT factorization
Previous revision of QA_GENERATOR_REACHABILITY.md framed the mod-24
result as a counterexample to the mod-9 connectivity finding. That
framing was methodologically wrong: raw mod-24 component counts are not
comparable to mod-9 counts without a specified compatible morphism.

Retracted and replaced with the correct structural statement:

  Proposition (CRT factorization of {σ,μ} orbits; empirically verified
  for m ∈ {9, 24}):
    Let m = a·b with gcd(a,b) = 1. Under the CRT isomorphism
    (Z/mZ)² ≅ (Z/aZ)² × (Z/bZ)², the generators σ and μ act
    coordinate-wise, so the {σ,μ}-orbit decomposition factors as the
    Cartesian product of the per-factor decompositions.

Empirical: mod 24 = 8·3 has 8 components under {σ,μ}, and each is
exactly a (mod-3 component, mod-8 component) CRT pair — no exceptions.
Sizes factor: {2 × 4} pairs with product sizes matching 24-component
sizes exactly. Sum = 576 = 24².

Semiconjugacy: δ_A (componentwise qa_mod_9 reduction) fails σ
semiconjugacy (276/576) — it discards the mod-8 factor. δ_B
(componentwise qa_mod_3) semiconjugates both generators but projects
to mod 3 where class structure is degenerate.

"Class shift" retracted as a phenomenon — it was exactly the projection
artifact of δ_A dropping mod-8 information, as ChatGPT predicted.

mod 9 = 3² has a single prime power so CRT gives no factorization;
this is why {σ,μ} on mod 9 yields one Cosmos component. mod 24 = 8·3
splits. No contradiction — different moduli, same proposition.

Open: formal proof of the proposition, verification on m ∈ {15, 45, 72},
whether ν has a universal definition across m.

content:
docs(theory): extend reachability — mod-24 decomposes into 6 components
Follow-up to the ν reachability finding, testing whether the "full within-
Cosmos connectivity under {σ, μ}" result generalizes beyond mod 9. It does
not.

Measured on mod 24 directly:
  cosmos = 567, satellite = 8, singularity = 1.
  σ alone: 28 components on Cosmos (mix of period-24 + smaller).
  {σ, μ}:   6 components on Cosmos — sizes {384, 96, 48, 24, 12, 3}.

So from (1,1), {σ, μ} reaches 384/567 ≈ 68% of Cosmos, with 183 states
in 5 other components unreachable from this seed. The "full within-class
connectivity" property is mod-9 specific, not a general QA invariant.

ν is also mod-9 specific: ν = componentwise multiply by 5 works on mod 9
because 5 = 2⁻¹ (mod 9). On mod 24, gcd(2,24)=2 so 2 has no inverse and
the "halving" interpretation does not transfer.

What IS general: σ, μ, and any ν-analogue preserve the 3-class partition
(Cosmos / Satellite / Singularity). Reachability is within-class.

Pushback on the "QA Connectivity Theorem (candidate)" framing: elevation
to theorem was premature on mod-9-only data. Doc now states the corrected
scope explicitly and flags open questions: structure of the 6 mod-24
components, candidate bridging generators, and whether the mod-9
connectivity relates to 9 = 3² having a single prime factor.

Completes the QA-native architecture cert triple for the GLM-5 failure-mode
class (arXiv:2602.15763, Feb 2026). Each cert validates a QA-native
construction that eliminates a specific failure mode by construction, not
a patch on top of a continuous-learning substrate.

[257] QA Integer-State Pipeline — two-boundary-crossing invariant of
Theorem NT as a structural pipeline property. Observer/QA boundary crossed
exactly twice (input tokenization + output decoding); interior state is
integer tuples throughout; no re-tokenization through a continuous
intermediate. Eliminates the TITO misalignment failure mode (§4.1.2).
Validator independently recomputes a canonical 7-step trace from 5 seed
tokens and verifies bitwise determinism. Gates ISP_1/BOUND/INT/
NO_REPROJECT/DET/SRC/WIT/F.

[258] QA Generator-Pattern Training — training as discrete search over
integer generator space. Trained object is an integer tuple (b_0, e_0)
identified via exhaustive enumeration over m^2=81 starting tuples on S_9,
scored by exact orbit-trace match count. No gradients, no float optimizer
state (no Adam m/v buffers), no importance sampling, no policy-version
staleness, no trust region, no optimizer reset. Eliminates the async-RL
policy-staleness failure class (§4.1.2). Validator independently
recomputes identify_generator on canonical target trace and verifies the
declared trained_generator matches. Gates GPT_1/INT_GEN/NO_GRAD/DISCRETE/
ORBIT_EVAL/DET/SRC/WIT/F.

scope are revised.

Widened Phase 4.7 items

    only). Now: authority-differential cap, GENERAL — same 10:5 primary:
    internal gap that blocks C03/C07 also blocks T2 (head-to-head) and
    likely a portion of P-category misses. Triggers include contradicts-
    edge, provenance-chain membership, and lexically-tight internal
    observations.

         decision-matrix revision).
    3b  T2 is authority overweighting (same mechanism as C03/C07); fully
         covered by widened item 2. No separate fixture fix required.

New Limitations section (5 items)
  1. Q1 factor_dominance tests single-factor dominance (>80% / >50%); the
     actual picture is two-factor (authority 0.744 + prov_decay 0.218 =
     0.962). Q1 PASS holds per prereg letter; "ranker well-tuned" is not
     fully supported by the evidence alone.
  2. Authority @3 = 8/8 is partly lexical-tautology on curator-specified
     gold. Validates candidate-pool surfacing, not authority-over-lexical
     discrimination. v3 I3 review flagged; prereg honoured anyway.
  3. Gate 2 at boundary (6/8). C03/C07 share T2's mechanism; one more
     miss in Beta-C flips gate 2 to FAIL. Authority-differential work
     must ship before Beta-C.
  4. Gate 6 conflates T1 (framing) with T2 (ranker). Split into 3a/3b.
  5. α=1.5 "Pareto-optimal" holds per prereg axes only; α=1.75/2.0
     dominate on rank-position signals (3 of 8 pairs shift from rank
     2/3 to rank 1/2 under stronger boost).

Full α ablation table

author: Player2
content:
feat(qa-kg): Phase 6 — MCP agent-write surface + [255] — ALPHA BAR
Phase 6 of QA-MEM ships the agent-integration firewall: a stdlib-only
stdio MCP JSON-RPC server (no `mcp` SDK dep) with EXACTLY four tools, a
capability mask that can hide the write tool entirely from a session's
tools/list, per-session rate limit under flock on a dedicated
_agent_writes.json ledger, tamper-evident mcp_session provenance on
every promote, immutable authority (both directions), audit-log
coverage on every tool call, and two forensic detectors in the
security audit.

MCP surface (tools/qa_kg_mcp/):
- qa_kg_search  (READ_ONLY)  - kg.search_authority_ranked delegation
- qa_kg_get_node  (READ_ONLY)  - single-node + epistemic fields lookup
- qa_kg_neighbors  (READ_ONLY)  - edges in/out/both
- qa_kg_promote_agent_note  (READ_WRITE)  - kg.promote() escalation
Capability declared at spawn via `--cap {read_only,read_write}`; agent
cannot self-elevate. READ_ONLY session's tools/list OMITS promote.

Agent-write path is exclusively the extractor + promote — the MCP
server never creates nodes. AgentNote rows are produced ONLY by
tools/qa_kg/extractors/agent_notes.py from collab-bus events / OB
thoughts with originSessionId / collab_log_activity rows.

Plan v2 resolutions folded in (per memory/project_qa_mem_review_role.md):
- M1: _agent_writes.json is a parallel file to _meta_ledger.json, own
  flock, zero shared state between meta_validator and rate_limit.
- M2: authority immutable BOTH directions — agent->internal upgrade AND
  primary->agent silent downgrade both raise FirewallViolation
  ("authority_immutable"). Real corrections require delete+recreate.
- M3: broadcast_payload deep-copied in the server, then mcp_session
  stamped AFTER the copy so any agent-provided spoofed key is
  overwritten, not preserved. W3b detector flags edges missing stamp.
- S1: counter decays on explicit session_done only; crashed sessions
  recovered via `python -m tools.qa_kg_mcp.cli reset-writes <session>`.
  No polling, no inactivity timer.
- S2: alpha-bar flip matches the original three-cert bar
  ([228]+[254]+[255]). Phases 4.5 / 5.1 are operational hardening, not
  alpha-bar items. QA-MEM is now alpha agent memory + authoritative
  project memory from this commit forward.
- N1: W6 subprocess test uses try/finally with terminate -> wait ->
  kill escalation. No fixture teardown.

New cert [255] QA_KG_AGENT_WRITE_SURFACE_CERT.v1 (all HARD):
- W1 AST scan — TOOL_SCHEMAS has exactly 4 tool names; no Node
  constructor or .upsert_node callsite inside tools/qa_kg_mcp/.
- W2 kg.upsert_node(authority='agent',...) callsites confined to
  tools/qa_kg/extractors/agent_notes.py + tools/qa_kg/tests/ +
  qa_alphageometry_ptolemy/qa_kg_*_cert_v*/ (legitimate fixtures).
- W3a qa_security_audit.check_qa_kg_db_direct_writes scans
  llm_qa_wrapper/ledger/enforced.jsonl for direct sqlite3 writes to
  qa_kg.db. W3 lives in the audit layer because
  llm_qa_wrapper/cert_gate_hook.py is WRAPPER_SELF_MODIFICATION-
  protected. Fixture injects synthetic ledger entry; detector must flag.
- W3b qa_security_audit.check_mcp_provenance scans promoted-from edges
  for missing mcp_session marker in broadcast_payload_snapshot.
  Fixture inserts one without the marker; detector must flag.
- W4 rate_limit.increment raises RateLimitExceeded at cap+1 with the
  session id in the message.
- W5 authority immutable both directions (fixtures: agent->internal,
  primary->agent, same-auth idempotent).
- W6 READ_ONLY server's tools/list excludes promote; READ_WRITE exposes
  all 4; tools/call promote under READ_ONLY returns JSON-RPC -32601.
- W7 every MCP tool call writes to query_log (reads rank>=0, writes
  rank=-1 sentinel). Fixture exercises all 4 tools in one session and
  asserts row shape.
- W8 no except-Exception-pass / bare except: pass swallows in
  tools/qa_kg_mcp/ or tools/qa_kg/_audit.py (AST scan, mirrors [228]
  D7 / [254] R8 / [225] KG10).

Compatibility:
- Schema v4 stays. No new columns. No new tables. query_log reused as
  the audit surface; pre-Phase-6 consumers filter rank >= 0 and are
  unaffected.
- _agent_writes.json is additive, gitignored, own flock.
- kg.promote() grew two optional kwargs (mcp_session,
  agent_writes_path); extractor callers pass neither, counter is not
  touched.

Validation:
- [255] W1-W8 all PASS standalone.
- qa_meta_validator.py full sweep: 210 families PASS (was 209, +[255]).
- [228] expected_hash.json refreshed once — legitimate intentional
  refresh per cert README because Phase 6 adds a new
  qa_alphageometry_ptolemy/qa_kg_*_cert_v1/ directory that the
  certs.py extractor now emits a Cert node for, shifting the fixture
  rebuild hash. All [228] gates PASS on the new hash.
- Tests: 38/38 kg_basic PASS (6 new Phase 6), 9/9 rate_limit PASS,

- Axiom linter CLEAN.
- Security audit: 12 PASS + 2 WARN + 1 pre-existing unrelated FAIL.
